_readme: PreloadPlugin config. Restart the server after making changes.
_backend_info: Where zone/cache data is saved. Options: JSON (flat files, easiest setup), SQLITE (single file DB, faster), MYSQL (requires separate DB server)
_bytesPerChunk_info: Estimated memory per loaded chunk in bytes. Used to calculate ACTUAL RAM in the zone table. Default 98304 = 96 KB (64 KB base × 1.5 overhead).
_startupLoadStrategy_info: How zones are loaded when the server boots. STAGED = load gradually to reduce lag spike. IMMEDIATE = load everything at once.
_suppressRamWarnings_info: Set true to silence warnings if PreloadPlugin's RAM usage looks high. Leave false unless the warnings are false positives.
_snapshotIntervalMinutes_info: How often (in minutes) a full cache snapshot is written to disk. Lower = less data lost on crash, higher = less disk I/O.
_rebuildFromChunksOnCorruption_info: If the cache file is corrupted on startup, automatically rebuild it from Hytale's raw chunk files instead of starting empty.
_scheduledRebuildIntervalHours_info: How often (in hours) to do a full cache rebuild from chunk files as a health-check. Set to 0 to disable. Default: every 24h.
_defaults_info: Default settings applied to all zones unless overridden per-zone.

defaults._strategy_info: WARM = chunks held in memory permanently (fast, uses more RAM). PREDICTIVE = chunks loaded on player approach and cached (balanced).
defaults._warmRadius_info: How many chunks out from the zone centre to keep loaded (radius, not diameter). E.g. 4 = 9x9 chunk area.
defaults._warmRadiusX_info: Override warmRadius for the X axis only. Set to null to use warmRadius for both axes.
defaults._warmRadiusZ_info: Override warmRadius for the Z axis only. Set to null to use warmRadius for both axes.
defaults._timeoutSeconds_info: How long (seconds) to wait for a zone to finish loading before giving up and letting the player through anyway.
defaults._bufferSeconds_info: How long a zone stays in each tier before being allowed to drop down to COLD. Prevents thrashing on a busy server.

_activation_info: Controls when PreloadPlugin decides a player is 'approaching' a zone and should trigger a pre-load.

activation._distance_info: Horizontal distance (in blocks/units) at which a nearby zone is considered 'approached' and pre-loading begins.
activation._distanceVertical_info: Vertical distance for the same check. Smaller than horizontal because players usually approach portals head-on.
activation._shape_info: Shape of the detection volume around the zone. ELLIPSOID = oval (smoother), CYLINDER = flat circle, BOX = rectangular.
activation._floorCeilingCheck_info: If true, players standing directly above or below a zone won't trigger it — only players at roughly the same Y level.
activation._facingCheck_info: If true, only trigger pre-loading if the player is actually facing toward the zone (reduces false triggers).
activation._cooldownSeconds_info: After triggering a zone pre-load, wait this many seconds before the same zone can be triggered again. Prevents spam.
activation._commitWindowSeconds_info: A player must be within range for this many seconds continuously before the pre-load actually starts. Filters brief walk-bys.
activation._predictiveRadius_info: Radius (in chunks) for predictive pre-loading when a player approaches a portal. Matches Hytale's simulation distance max (7).

_ttl_info: How long (in days) each cache tier is kept before being discarded. -1 means keep forever.

ttl._warm_info: WARM zones (permanently held in RAM). -1 = never expire.
ttl._recentHot_info: Zones that were recently HOT (a player was just there). Kept for 7 days by default.
ttl._predictive_info: Zones pre-loaded by prediction. Shorter TTL since they may never be visited.
ttl._lowTraffic_info: Zones flagged as low-traffic (below lowTrafficThreshold). Expire quickly to free RAM.
ttl._bed_info: Cached bed/spawn locations. Kept for 3 days.
ttl._deathLocation_info: Cached player death locations. Only need to persist for one session.

cache._persistColdCache_info: Save COLD-tier chunk geometry to disk so it survives server restarts. Strongly recommended.
cache._cacheDirectory_info: Folder (relative to the plugin directory) where cache files are stored.
cache._maxCacheAgeDays_info: Delete cache files older than this many days on startup to prevent unbounded disk growth.
cache._predictiveCacheTTLDays_info: How long predictive-cache files are kept on disk (should match ttl.predictive above).
cache._startupLoadStrategy_info: Overrides the top-level startupLoadStrategy for the disk cache specifically. Usually keep these in sync.
cache._ownershipAuditIntervalMinutes_info: How often (in minutes) to audit chunk ownership for drift (chunks evicted by the engine without OptiPortal noticing). Default: 5.

_stagedLoadConcurrency_info: Number of WARM zones loaded concurrently at startup. Higher = faster startup but more world-thread pressure. Default: 3.
_lowTrafficThreshold_info: If a zone has fewer than this many active players, it's considered 'low traffic' and uses a shorter TTL (see ttl.lowTraffic).
_immuneToSimulationReduction_info: If true, PreloadPlugin-managed zones are never unloaded by Hytale's own simulation distance reduction. Keeps them stable.
_chunkPressure_info: Hard abort: if the world already has this many chunks loaded, OptiPortal will not load more. Set to -1 to disable. Raise this if you see 'chunk pressure limit' warnings caused by permanently-warm zones keeping the count high.
_keepalive_info: Periodically 'pings' zones to prevent them dropping to a lower tier. Useful for warps or hubs you want always-ready.

keepalive._hot_info: Keep HOT zones hot (re-ping before they cool down).
keepalive._cold_info: Bring COLD zones back to WARM proactively. Disabled by default — enable for critical always-on zones.

_decay_info: Controls how quickly zones drop from HOT → WARM → COLD when no players are present.

decay._hotDecaySeconds_info: Seconds after a player leaves before a HOT zone starts cooling toward WARM.
decay._warmDecayMinutes_info: Minutes a zone spends in WARM before dropping to COLD.
decay._pollIntervalSeconds_info: How often (seconds) the decay system checks all zones. Lower = more responsive, slightly more CPU.

_async_info: Server load sensing: monitors TPS and adjusts chunk loading behavior to reduce load during server stress.

async._tpsMonitorEnabled_info: Enable TPS monitoring. When enabled, OptiPortal will reduce chunk loading when server TPS drops.
async._tpsLowThreshold_info: TPS below this value triggers reduced batch sizes. Default: 15.0.
async._tpsCriticalThreshold_info: TPS below this value queues all non-critical operations. Default: 12.0.
async._maxLoadedChunksPressureThreshold_info: If ChunkStore.getLoadedChunksCount() exceeds this across any world, batch size is reduced proportionally. Default: 2000. -1 = disabled.

_warps_info: Point this at your server's warps.json so PreloadPlugin can pre-load warp destinations automatically.

warps._watchForChanges_info: Automatically reload the warp list when the file changes (e.g. a new warp is added) without restarting.
warps._fields_info: The field names in your warps.json. Adjust if your warp plugin uses different keys.

_gravestones_info: Reads the Gravestones mod's data file so PreloadPlugin can pre-load the area around each gravestone.

gravestone._enabled_info: Enable the Gravestones plugin integration. Requires the Gravestones plugin to be installed.
gravestone._releaseOnBreak_info: Stop keeping the gravestone's chunk loaded once the gravestone block is broken.
gravestone._releaseOnEmpty_info: Stop keeping the gravestone's chunk loaded once the gravestone is emptied (all items collected).

_section_mysql: === MYSQL (only used if backend is set to MYSQL above) ===
_ui_info: Controls what's shown in the PreloadPlugin admin UI panel.

ui._showInstancedPortals_info: Show instanced (per-player) portals in the zone table.
ui._showBedSpawns_info: Show player bed/spawn cache entries in the UI.
ui._showDeathLocations_info: Show cached death locations in the UI.

_metrics_info: Anonymous usage stats sent to bStats (bstats.org). Helps the developer see how the plugin is used.

metrics._bstatsPluginId_info: Your plugin's bStats ID. Leave 0 if you haven't registered it yet.

updateChecker._enabled_info: Check for new OptiPortal versions on startup and notify in the console.

portalLinks._confidenceThreshold_info: Number of observations required before a portal link is considered confirmed.
